expectations.text: The review does NOT flag a dependency direction violation — the adapter correctly imports from domain and usecase, not the other way around
expectations.evidence: Report contains no dependency direction or architectural boundary false positives. No mention of incorrect imports.

expectations.text: The review does NOT flag the unexported helper functions (normalizeEmail, isValidEmail, generateID) as issues — they are correctly unexported
expectations.evidence: Report does not flag any unexported functions. No false positive about visibility.

expectations.text: The review recognizes the interface-based DI pattern as correct (UserRepository and Notifier interfaces in usecase, implemented by adapter)
expectations.evidence: Report does not flag the DI pattern as problematic. No false positive about interfaces or injection. The nil-notifier issue is a legitimate concern about nil-safety, not about the DI pattern itself.

expectations.text: The review notes the placeholder generateID() implementation as incomplete work or flags the comment
expectations.evidence: Report [Important]: 'generateID() always returns the same ID, causing duplicate primary keys across registrations... returns constant "usr_placeholder".'

expectations.text: The report still performs standard checks (error handling, edge cases) and doesn't only focus on boundary issues
expectations.evidence: Report covers nil notifier panic risk, missing test coverage for success/failure/edge paths — standard code review concerns beyond boundary items.
